title: Troubleshooting CSS Caching in Spring Boot

message.text: Hey! I am writing a Spring Boot application. Why when I change the styles.css file and run the application my styles do not apply and the app still uses the old style.css?

editAgentRounds.reply:
Here’s a quick plan to diagnose why the updated CSS isn’t being served.  
Checklist:
- Confirm which `style.css` the app is actually serving
- Check browser cache behavior and disable/clear it
- Verify Spring Boot static resource caching/devtools settings
- Confirm build/run flow doesn’t package old resources

Most likely cause is caching (browser or Spring). A few focused checks:

- **Confirm the served file**: Open DevTools → Network → reload → click `style.css` and check the response content. If it’s old, the server is serving the old file; if it’s new but not reflected, it’s likely cache or selector specificity.
- **Hard-refresh / disable cache**: In DevTools Network tab, check “Disable cache” and reload. If it fixes it, it was browser cache.  
- **Make sure you edited the right file**: In your workspace there’s also `target/` and `bin/` copies. You want to edit `src/main/resources/static/css/style.css` only. If you edited a `target` copy, it will be overwritten.
- **Static resource caching**: Spring can cache static resources. If you have caching enabled in `application.properties`, disable it for dev. Look for properties like `spring.web.resources.cache.*`.
- **Running from an old build**: If you run a packaged JAR, it may still contain the old CSS. For dev, run from source (e.g., `mvn spring-boot:run`) and do a clean build if needed.

If you want, I can check your `application.properties` or confirm which CSS file is served by inspecting the HTML templates.

turns.suggestedTitle: Troubleshooting CSS Caching in Spring Boot
